channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louisiana hunter cited for violations after shooting man he mistook for a deer
item.description: A Louisiana hunter was cited for two violations after accidentally shooting another hunter, who he mistook for a deer, while they were both on the same "small" property.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NFC's No. 1 seed comes down to Vikings-Lions showdown at Detroit in Week 18
item.description: The NFC’s No. 1 seed will come down to the final game of the regular season when the Detroit Lions host the Minnesota Vikings next Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: Brace for turbulence: Lessons from a bumpy 'super year' of global elections
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When voters around the globe had their say in 2024, their message was often: “You’re fired.” Some 70 countries that are home to half the world’s population held elections this year, and in many incumbents were punished.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter: A brief bio
item.description: — BIRTH NAME: James Earl Carter, Jr. — BORN: Oct. 1, 1924, at the Wise Clinic in Plains, Georgia, the first U.S. president born in a hospital. He would become the first president to live for an entire century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Charles Shyer, ‘Father of the Bride’ and ‘Baby Boom’ filmmaker, dies at 83
item.description: Charles Shyer, the Oscar-nominated writer and filmmaker known for classic comedies like “Private Benjamin,” “Baby Boom” and “Father of the Bride” that he made alongside Nancy Meyers, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, WGN 9

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, WGN 9

item.title: Missing men searching for Sasquatch die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: The Tesla CEO said he has the “right” to address the country’s political climate as he has “made significant investments” in Germany’s technological and industrial sectors.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, ABC 4

item.title: Trump applauds blocked sale of Texas border wall materials
item.description: President-elect Trump celebrated the blocked sale of border wall materials following a Friday ruling from a federal judge in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: This Utah city is among America's top 5 safest small towns: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oldest living survivor of attack on Pearl Harbor dies at 105
item.description: Warren Upton, the oldest living survivor of the 1941 Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor and the last remaining survivor of the USS Utah, has died at age 105.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's how a robot massage compares to the real thing
item.description: First, you book an appointment through an app - it's sort of like getting an Uber. You pick a time and place, and it's all confirmed on the spot. A half-hour massage is $60 at the location I visited.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: Will New Year's Eve be loud or quiet? What are the top 2025 resolutions? AP-NORC poll has answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you're planning on ringing in the new year quietly at home, you're not alone. A majority of U.S adults intend to celebrate New Year’s Eve at home, according to a new poll by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Mega Millions player in California is $1.22B richer
item.description: After no players won on Christmas Eve, the Mega Millions lottery jackpot ballooned to $1.22 billion. The winning ticket was sold Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Vote to name new baby pygmy hippo at Metro Richmond Zoo
item.description: The Metro Richmond Zoo is asking for the public's help to name its new baby pygmy hippo that the zoo welcomed just before the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Marysa Tuttle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More than a third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in debt this holiday season: report
item.description: (KRON) -- More than one third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in credit card debt this holiday season, according to a new report. The report from LendingTree indicates that 36% of Americans took on an average of $1,181 in debt, up from $1,028 in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe survives close brush with the sun's scorching surface
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NASA's Parker Solar Probe has successfully made the closest approach to the sun, the space agency confirmed Friday. Earlier this week, the spacecraft passed within a record-breaking 3.8 million miles (6 million kilometers) of the scorching star.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
